strengths: Roughly 17+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, satisfying the 15-year experience minimum
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption (Green Map)
Active knowledge-sharer with conference talks, multi-day courses, and workshops, loosely matching the 'inspire others' responsibility
concerns: AI exposure is shallow and very recent — about one year of contributing to MCP tooling and agent infrastructure, with no recognized AI expertise, no shipped AI products or platforms, and no ML depth as the role's preferred profile demands

No principal-, director-, or strategy-level track record: every role is senior/staff IC delivery work, with no evidence of setting cloud/product strategy, advising strategic customers, or leading disruptive initiatives at organizational scale

Education and career shape misfit — bachelor's degrees only (preferred: Master's/PhD in an AI field), frequent 1–2 year tenures, and deep investment in niche ecosystems (DLang, Ember.js) rather than cloud platform leadership

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise or AI-built products

response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile senior engineer, but the role calls for a director-level AI strategist with recognized AI credentials, demonstrated AI products, and a history of shaping cloud strategy and advising strategic customers. His AI involvement spans roughly one year of agent-tooling contributions, his leadership evidence is limited to workshops and a small open-source project, and his career has been IC feature delivery in mid-sized companies — often in niche technologies far from the cloud/AI platform mainstream. Against the core requirements of this Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) position, he presents as a weak fit.
